skill.name: glm4v-analyze-image
skill.description: Use GLM-4V Flash to analyze image URLs, describe visual content, answer questions, and extract visible text.
skill.summary: Analyze images and answer visual questions with GLM-4V Flash.
skill.author: CK991357

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution, weak-cryptography, and entropy findings appear to be false positives from Markdown code fences, model names, and example URLs in SKILL.md. The skill still has a real network and privacy consideration because it instructs users to analyze publicly reachable image URLs through an external vision model.

medium_findings.title: External Image URL Processing
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to provide image_url values and requires images to be publicly accessible. This is expected for a vision-analysis skill, but users could expose private images or sensitive visual data to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented parameters and examples consistently require image URLs. The risk is about user data exposure through intended network use, not confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Finding Is a Markdown False Positive
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are fenced json examples and inline Markdown formatting. No executable script, shell command, or Ruby code is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows fenced JSON examples, not backtick command substitution. There is no evidence of command execution intent.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is a Token-Matching False Positive
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography hits correspond to descriptive metadata, the glm-4v-flash model name, and sample image URLs. No hash function, cipher, or cryptographic operation is defined.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain model and documentation text only. I did not find evidence of cryptographic API use or weak algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Finding Is Not Supported by File Content
low_findings.description: SKILL.md is a readable Markdown guide with Chinese prose and JSON examples. No binary blob, encrypted payload, encoded script, or hidden execution block is visible.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The entire file is legible documentation. The entropy heuristic likely reflects mixed-language text and structured examples rather than obfuscation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Public URL Requirement for Image Analysis
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks users to ensure image URLs are publicly accessible before analysis. This enables normal operation but can lead to unintended disclosure if private images are exposed through shareable URLs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The public URL requirement is explicit in the documentation. The concern depends on user-provided content and is not evidence of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Teams need fast visual understanding without writing custom multimodal prompts each time. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a clear GLM-4V image-analysis workflow for descriptions, questions, OCR, and detail extraction.
actual_capabilities: Formats calls to the glm4v_analyze_image tool with model, image_url, and prompt parameters.
Uses the glm-4v-flash model for documented image-analysis requests.
Supports image description, visual question answering, and detail-focused analysis prompts.
Guides OCR-style extraction of visible text from images.
Documents supported formats such as JPEG, PNG, and WebP.
limitations: Requires an accessible image URL rather than a local image path.
Does not support real-time video streams or 3D model analysis.
Image size, resolution, and service limits may affect results.
Sensitive or inappropriate images should not be submitted.

use_cases.title: Review Product Images
use_cases.description: Describe objects, visible text, colors, and layout details from product or catalog images.

use_cases.title: Answer Visual Questions
use_cases.description: Ask targeted questions about people, objects, actions, and relationships visible in an image.

use_cases.title: Extract Visual Evidence
use_cases.description: Summarize screenshots, diagrams, or photos before creating reports or support notes.

prompt_templates.title: Describe an Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this image URL with GLM-4V Flash. Describe the main subjects, setting, colors, and visible text in clear sentences.

prompt_templates.title: Answer Specific Questions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GLM-4V Flash to inspect this image. Answer these questions: what objects are present, what are they doing, and what details support the answer?

prompt_templates.title: Extract Text and Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this screenshot. Extract visible text, identify interface elements, and summarize the layout from top to bottom.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Visual Details
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the image in stages. First list observable facts, then infer context, then note uncertain areas and details that need verification.

output_examples.input: A product photo URL with a request to describe packaging and visible text.
output_examples.output: The response describes the package shape, dominant colors, readable label text, and visible product features.

output_examples.input: A screenshot URL with a question about page state and interface controls.
output_examples.output: The response identifies the main screen, active controls, visible messages, and any text that appears in the screenshot.

output_examples.input: A scene photo URL with a request for object and activity recognition.
output_examples.output: The response lists visible people, objects, actions, spatial relationships, and uncertain observations separately.

best_practices: Use a direct, accessible image URL and avoid private or sensitive images.
Write specific prompts that name the objects, text, or details you need.
Ask for uncertainty notes when the image is small, blurry, or visually complex.
anti_patterns: Do not submit confidential screenshots, identity documents, or private photos.
Do not use vague prompts when exact visual details are required.
Do not treat visual inferences as verified facts without independent review.

faq.question: What model does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill documents use of the glm-4v-flash model for image analysis.

faq.question: Can it analyze local image files?
faq.answer: The documented workflow uses image URLs. Local files must be made accessible through a suitable upload or hosting process.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill mentions common formats such as JPEG, PNG, and WebP.

faq.question: Can it read text in images?
faq.answer: Yes. The guide lists visible text recognition and OCR-style extraction as supported capabilities.

faq.question: Is it safe for private images?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill requires accessible image URLs and should not be used with private or sensitive images.

faq.question: Does it support video analysis?
faq.answer: No. The documented limitations say real-time video streams are not supported.
